channel.title: Neo4j — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Neo4j (neo4j).

item.title: CVE-2026-1471 · 2.1 LOW — Caching of authentication context
item.description: Excessive caching of authentication context in Neo4j Enterprise edition versions prior to 2026.01.4 leads to authenticated users inheriting the context of the first user who authenticated after restart. The issue is limited to certain non-default con…

item.title: CVE-2026-1524 · 2.1 LOW — Auth misconfiguration when multiple providers enabled
item.description: An edgecase in SSO implementation in Neo4j Enterprise edition versions prior to version 2026.02 can lead to unauthorised access under the following conditions: If a neo4j admin configures two or more OIDC providers AND configures one or more of them…

item.title: CVE-2026-1497 · 2 LOW — Incorrect privilege assignment in composite databases
item.description: Incorrect resolving of namespaces in composite databases in Neo4j Enterprise edition prior to versions 2026.02 and 5.26.22 can lead to the following scenario: an admin that intends to give a user an access to a remote database constituent "namespace.…

item.title: CVE-2026-1337 · 1.1 LOW — Insufficient escaping of unicode characters in query log
item.description: Insufficient escaping of unicode characters in query log in Neo4j Enterprise and Community editions prior to 2026.01 can lead to XSS if the user opens the logs in a tool that treats them as HTML. There is no security impact on Neo4j products, but thi…

item.title: CVE-2026-1622 · 4.8 MEDIUM — Unredacted data exposure in query.log
item.description: Neo4j Enterprise and Community editions versions prior to 2026.01.3 and 5.26.21 are vulnerable to a potential information disclosure by a user who has ability to access the local log files. The "obfuscate_literals" option in the query logs does not r…

item.title: CVE-2025-12738 · 1.3 LOW — Enumeration of restricted property value
item.description: Neo4j Enterprise edition versions prior to 2025.11.2 and 5.26.17 are vulnerable to a potential information disclosure by an attacker who has some legitimate access to the database. The vulnerability allows attacker without read access to a property t…

item.title: CVE-2025-11602 · 6.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Untargeted information leak in Bolt protocol handshake
item.description: Potential information leak in bolt protocol handshake in Neo4j Enterprise and Community editions allows attacker to obtain one byte of information from previous connections. The attacker has no control over the information leaked in server responses.

item.title: CVE-2025-10193 · 7.4 HIGH 🌩️ — Neo4j Cypher MCP server is vulnerable to DNS rebinding attacks
item.description: DNS rebinding vulnerability in Neo4j Cypher MCP server allows malicious websites to bypass Same-Origin Policy protections and execute unauthorised tool invocations against locally running Neo4j MCP instances. The attack relies on the user being entic…
